Robert N. Tooker, 427 Fourteenth
avenue, is the first citizen, and the
North Hill improvement club 10 the
first civic welfare organization, to
take up arms against the Iaxity Of
the city officials in permitting a
water shortage such as has existed
in Spokane for the last three
months, and to demand that the
whole are partial return Of water
taxes be made to those who have
paid for the precious fluid but have
not been given II.


In the letter Tooker makes formal
application for a return of 50 Of his
water tax money. 1t is expected
that his letter will be followed by
many other citizens from all Sec
tons of the city who have had SIM
liar or more serious experiences.


ON NORTH HILL.


The situation is getting past the
"Smite-thee-on-one-cheek-and turn
the-other" stage for many persons
and the protests which have hith-
erto been verbal or literal probably
hereafter will be legal. The North
Hill Improvement club addressed a
communication last night to the
city council, which reached the Of
flee of City Clerk Fleming this
morning, which demands a volun-
tary refund of water taxes on the
part of the city. Vigorous protest is
also made against the inadequacy
of the fire protection in certain Sec
tons.


Through the president, E. ]. Tam.
bin, and the secretary, James New
lands, the North Hill Improvement
club. says:


THAT, whereas, the recent water
shortage has caused intense suffer.
ing to many persons and has caused
considerable damage to property
and, whereas, much labor and time
has been spent in keeping the vege.
tation from being entirely dried up
and, whereas, the city has entered
into contractural relation with the
citizens to supply water at a certain
fixed charge, and whereas such
water has not been delivered any
where near the terms of the agree
ment, we ask that the city council
instruct the proper officials to re
turn to the citizens who have paiG
for water but have not received II
the full amount of lawn sprinklins
rent, and from 25 to rs per cent Of
the general water rent, according tC
the elevation Of the party's grounds
and the service rendered"


TIL city council is thus asked tt
take action which will mean the
voluntary payment of thousands o.
rebates. Whether or not the city
council will act as directed will no.
be determined until the next regu
lar meeting.
